label: anti-fab-stage2
mutation_summary: Added explicit anti-fabrication rule covering concept names, theses, hooks, and SEO keyword lists. Rule forbids substituting an inferred proper-noun incumbent name when the brief did not supply one. Examples cite 'We Quit Sentry' as a violation.

failures.c6: Stage 4 SEO keyword list contains 'sentry alternative under 100 per seat' — a fabricated proper-noun brand token inherited from the category description. Concept names and theses are clean; the leak migrated to the SEO sub-section despite the mutation rule explicitly covering SEO keyword lists. Same standard as baseline scoring.

verdict_reasoning: Aggregate total strictly improves (16 → 17). Target criterion c6 improved as hypothesized (1/3 → 2/3). No per-criterion regression. The one remaining c6 failure shows the mutation worked at the locus it was written for (Stage 2 ideation), but the lowercase token 'sentry' leaked through Stage 4's SEO keyword generation — the rule was not enforced downstream. Iter 2 should target Stage 4 SEO sub-section specifically, or tighten the rule to forbid the banned token globally regardless of casing.
notes: Iter 1 is a partial success: the concept-naming locus is now clean across all 3 outputs (baseline had 'We Quit Sentry' and similar in Stage 2). The failure mode has migrated downstream rather than being eliminated. Worth noting that output 02's subagent self-reported 'anti-fabrication rule honored throughout' — but the actual text contained a violation. This confirms the importance of deterministic grep-based judging over trust-the-generator self-reports.
